en: Now even the first covenant had regulations for worship and an earthly place of holiness.
zh: 前約也有它敬拜的規例，和屬世界的聖所。

en: For a tent was prepared, the first section, in which were the lampstand and the table and the bread of the Presence. It is called the Holy Place.
zh: 因為有一個支搭好了的會幕，第一進叫作聖所，裡面有燈臺、桌子和陳設餅。

en: Behind the second curtain was a second section called the Most Holy Place,
zh: 在第二層幔子後面還有一個會幕，叫作至聖所，

en: having the golden altar of incense and the ark of the covenant covered on all sides with gold, in which was a golden urn holding the manna, and Aaron's staff that budded, and the tablets of the covenant.
zh: 裡面有金香壇，有全部包金的約櫃，櫃裡有盛著嗎哪的金罐、亞倫那發過芽的杖和兩塊約板。

en: Above it were the cherubim of glory overshadowing the mercy seat. Of these things we cannot now speak in detail.
zh: 櫃的上面有榮耀的基路伯罩著施恩座，關於這一切，現在不能一一細說了。

en: These preparations having thus been made, the priests go regularly into the first section, performing their ritual duties,
zh: 這一切物件都這樣預備好了，祭司就常常進入第一進會幕，執行敬拜的事。

en: but into the second only the high priest goes, and he but once a year, and not without taking blood, which he offers for himself and for the unintentional sins of the people.
zh: 至於第二進會幕，只有大祭司一年一次獨自進去，並且非帶著血不可，好為自己和人民的愚妄把血獻上。

en: By this the Holy Spirit indicates that the way into the holy places is not yet opened as long as the first section is still standing
zh: 聖靈藉著這事表明，當第一進會幕存在的時候，進入至聖所的路，還沒有顯明出來。

en: (which is symbolic for the present age). According to this arrangement, gifts and sacrifices are offered that cannot perfect the conscience of the worshiper,
zh: 這第一進會幕是現今的時代的預表，其實所獻的禮物和祭品，都不能使敬拜的人在良心上得到完全。

en: but deal only with food and drink and various washings, regulations for the body imposed until the time of reformation.
zh: 這些只是關於飲食和各樣潔淨的禮儀，是在“更新的時候”來到之前，為肉體立的規例。

en: But when Christ appeared as a high priest of the good things that have come, then through the greater and more perfect tent (not made with hands, that is, not of this creation)
zh: 但基督已經來了，作了已經實現的美好事物的大祭司；他經過更大、更完備的會幕 。

en: he entered once for all into the holy places, not by means of the blood of goats and calves but by means of his own blood, thus securing an eternal redemption.
zh: 他不是用山羊和牛犢的血，而是用自己的血，只一次進了至聖所，就得到了永遠的救贖。

en: For if the sprinkling of defiled persons with the blood of goats and bulls and with the ashes of a heifer sanctifies for the purification of the flesh,
zh: 如果山羊和公牛的血，以及母牛犢的灰，灑在不潔的人身上，尚且可以使他們成為聖潔，身體潔淨，

en: how much more will the blood of Christ, who through the eternal Spirit offered himself without blemish to God, purify our conscience from dead works to serve the living God.
zh: 何況基督的血呢？他藉著永遠的靈，把自己無瑕無疵的獻給 神，他的血不是更能潔淨我們的良心脫離致死的行為，使我們可以事奉永活的 神嗎？

en: Therefore he is the mediator of a new covenant, so that those who are called may receive the promised eternal inheritance, since a death has occurred that redeems them from the transgressions committed under the first covenant.
zh: 因此，他作了新約的中保，藉著他的死，使人在前約之下的過犯得到救贖，就叫那些蒙召的人，得著永遠基業的應許。

en: For where a will is involved, the death of the one who made it must be established.
zh: 凡有遺囑 ，必須證實立遺囑的人死了；

en: For a will takes effect only at death, since it is not in force as long as the one who made it is alive.
zh: 因為人死了，遺囑才能確立，立遺囑的人還活著的時候，遺囑決不生效。

en: Therefore not even the first covenant was inaugurated without blood.
zh: 因此，前約並不是沒有用血立的：

en: For when every commandment of the law had been declared by Moses to all the people, he took the blood of calves and goats, with water and scarlet wool and hyssop, and sprinkled both the book itself and all the people,
zh: 當日摩西按照律法，向所有人民宣布了各樣的誡命，就拿牛犢 的血和水，用朱紅色的羊毛與牛膝草，灑在律法書上和人民身上，

en: saying, "This is the blood of the covenant that God commanded for you."
zh: 說：“這就是 神規定你們立約的血。”

en: And in the same way he sprinkled with the blood both the tent and all the vessels used in worship.
zh: 他照樣把血灑在會幕和各樣應用的器皿上。

en: Indeed, under the law almost everything is purified with blood, and without the shedding of blood there is no forgiveness of sins.
zh: 按著律法，幾乎所有都是用血潔淨的，如果沒有流血，就沒有赦免。

en: Thus it was necessary for the copies of the heavenly things to be purified with these rites, but the heavenly things themselves with better sacrifices than these.
zh: 照著天上樣式作的既然必須這樣去潔淨，天上物體的本身，就應該用更美的祭品去潔淨了。

en: For Christ has entered, not into holy places made with hands, which are copies of the true things, but into heaven itself, now to appear in the presence of God on our behalf.
zh: 因為基督不是進了人手所做的聖所 ，而是進到天上，現在替我們顯露在 神的面前。

en: Nor was it to offer himself repeatedly, as the high priest enters the holy places every year with blood not his own,
zh: 他不必多次把自己獻上，好像大祭司每年帶著不是自己的血進入至聖所一樣。

en: for then he would have had to suffer repeatedly since the foundation of the world. But as it is, he has appeared once for all at the end of the ages to put away sin by the sacrifice of himself.
zh: 如果這樣，他從創世以來，就必須受許多次的苦了。可是現在他在這世代的終結，只顯現一次，把自己作為祭品獻上，好除掉罪。

en: And just as it is appointed for man to die once, and after that comes judgment,
zh: 按著定命，人人都要死一次，死後還有審判。

en: so Christ, having been offered once to bear the sins of many, will appear a second time, not to deal with sin but to save those who are eagerly waiting for him.
zh: 照樣，基督為了擔當許多人的罪，也曾經一次把自己獻上；將來他還要再一次顯現，不是為擔當罪，而是要向那些熱切期待他的人成全救恩。
